channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Foot Locker hace de la tecnología una capacidad empresarial
item.description: El comercio minorista es dinámico, está en constante cambio y genera una gran cantidad de datos; y a través de la comercialización, las transacciones en tienda, la cadena de suministro, la tecnología digital y la fijación de precios, las oportunidades para aprovechar los datos son infinitas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI 네이티브 소프트웨어 엔지니어링 시대, 생각보다 빨리 올 수도
item.description: 가트너의 소프트웨어 엔지니어링 부문 수석 책임 애널리스트인 필립 월시는 많은 개발 작업을 완전히 자동화하거나 부분적으로 도울 수 있는 AI 코딩 에이전트가 등장하고 있다며, 2025년에는 더 많은 파일럿 프로그램과 개념 증명이 출시될 것으로 예상된다고 말했다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 11 key roles for AI success
item.description: More companies in every industry are adopting AI to transform business processes. But the success of their initiatives depends on having the right people on board, not just data and technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Here’s how Cleary Gottlieb law firm uses genAI for pre-trial discovery and more
item.description: Corporate law is nothing like you see on television. To prepare for a case, 150 attorneys might be tasked to travel to remote warehouses to comb through tens of millions of documents gathering dust or track down amorphous electronic communications. It’s a process known as discovery.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Secure Azure Kubernetes with Advanced Container Networking Services
item.description: Microsoft’s Azure Kubernetes Service is designed to take care of much of the day-to-day management of Kubernetes, simplifying a lot of the complexity that goes with building and running large-scale, cloud-native applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access
item.description: How is cloud adoption transforming federal agencies and driving mission success? The post Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Why you shouldn’t store your money in payment apps
item.description: As use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Army modernization from the technology point of view
item.description: "We're working with our development teams and we're working with testing. And then we have a policy that covers all of that," said Young Bang. The post Army modernization from the technology point of view first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Derace Lauderdale, Federal News Network

item.title: Good customer experience isn’t just good. For federal agencies, it’s the law
item.description: "The purpose of the act is to improve government websites and its related services to enhance that customer experience," said GAO's Carol Harris. The post Good customer experience isn’t just good. For federal agencies, it’s the law first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Inside SSA’s plan to become the ‘most accessible agency’ in government
item.description: Betsy Beaumon, SSA’s chief transformation officer, says the is reinventing service delivery to keep up with a growing volume of customers. The post Inside SSA’s plan to become the ‘most accessible agency’ in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: It’s your last day to comment on one of the biggest cybersecurity rules ever
item.description: Final comments on a Defense Department cybersecurity rule are due today. The Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification project is about to become a reality. The post It’s your last day to comment on one of the biggest cybersecurity rules ever first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: The WordPress drama could have major implications for the internet
item.description: The WordPress situation has soured in the past several weeks, as a dispute spirals out of control between founder Matt Mullenweg and WP Engine, a platform that many users deploy in order to host their WordPress sites.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Google bets on nuclear power to drive AI expansion
item.description: Google has signed its first corporate deal to purchase power from multiple small modular reactors (SMRs) to meet the energy needs of its AI systems, marking a key step as AI companies shift toward nuclear power.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to manage generative AI programs – governance, education, regulation
item.description: Generative artificial intelligence has grown extremely rapidly, attracting investment and sparking developer interest and creativity. Companies see generative AI as a route to building innovative services and revolutionizing their industries.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: At the mercy of social media
item.description: I like Instagram as much as the next guy. I check in daily on my carefully curated feed of folks I follow and then work hard not to doomscroll the day away. One of my favorite follows is an adorable Corgi (aren’t they all adorable?) named Parsnip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Making generative AI work for you
item.description: It’s not often someone can talk about genAI in a “pragmatic and realistic” way, but those are exactly the accolades handed out to AWS Product Management Director Massimo Re Ferrè following his recent generative AI (genAI) talk. It’s not hard to find opposing sentiment.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ktor 3.0 switches to kotlinx.io library
item.description: Ktor 3.0, the latest version of the Kotlin-based framework for building asynchronous client- and server-side applications, is now available. Ktor 3.0 switches to the kotlinx.io library for handling data sources. The release also highlights support for server-sent events (SSE).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Will your AI bot put citizens at risk?
item.description: Government agencies have an obligation to provide accurate information to citizens, and a bad bot can have both legal and moral implications. The post Will your AI bot put citizens at risk? first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
